entries.translation_zh: 调查的结论是，停电并非源于单个重大错误，而是一连串小问题——每个单独看都容易处理——以无人预料的方式叠加所致。
entries.source_title: KeyLoop authored everyday English v1

entries.text: Far from being passive victims of globalization, the artisans documented in this ethnography actively reinterpret traditional crafts to appeal to international markets on their own terms.
entries.translation_zh: 这部民族志记录的手工艺人远非全球化的被动受害者，他们主动重新诠释传统工艺，以自己的方式吸引国际市场。
entries.source_title: KeyLoop authored everyday English v1

entries.text: The proposal to mine the seabed divides scientists: some see essential minerals for the energy transition, while others warn of destroying ecosystems we have barely begun to understand.
entries.translation_zh: 海底采矿的提议让科学家们意见分裂：一些人看到了能源转型所需的关键矿物，另一些人则警告这会摧毁我们才刚刚开始了解的生态系统。
entries.source_title: KeyLoop authored everyday English v1

entries.text: Reflecting on the failed reform, the minister admitted that the government had underestimated how deeply the existing system was woven into the daily routines of millions of households.
entries.translation_zh: 反思失败的改革时，部长承认政府低估了现行制度与千百万家庭日常生活交织的深度。
entries.source_title: KeyLoop authored everyday English v1

entries.text: The observatory's data archive, built with patience over half a century, proved decisive in confirming a deep physical effect that theory had predicted but no one had observed.
entries.translation_zh: 天文台耐心积累了半个世纪的数据档案，对证实理论预言却无人观测到的深层物理效应起了决定性作用。
entries.source_title: KeyLoop authored everyday English v1

entries.text: Whether the city's experiment in letting residents direct public spending truly shifts power, or merely dresses up decisions already made elsewhere, remains a matter of heated academic debate.
entries.translation_zh: 该市让居民支配公共开支的试验究竟是真正转移了权力，还是只为别处已做的决定披上外衣，仍是学界激烈争论的问题。
entries.source_title: KeyLoop authored everyday English v1

entries.text: There must be some misunderstanding between us.
entries.translation_zh: 我们之间肯定有误会。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: One of the children left the door open.
entries.translation_zh: 其中一个孩子让门开著。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Doing this is a lot of fun.
entries.translation_zh: 这样做很有趣。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I have been abroad several times this year.
entries.translation_zh: 我今年去了好几次外国。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: All of the cookies are in the shape of stars.
entries.translation_zh: 所有饼干都是星星的形状。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Will you help me give out the programs to those people?
entries.translation_zh: 你能帮我给这些人分发节目单吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She will accompany me on the piano.
entries.translation_zh: 她会弹钢琴为我伴奏。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I've lived away from home for a long time.
entries.translation_zh: 我在外地生活了很久。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There isn't any milk in the glass.
entries.translation_zh: 这个玻璃杯里没有任何的牛奶。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: This method is sure to work.
entries.translation_zh: 这个方法一定会成功。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: That red sweater looks good on you.
entries.translation_zh: 你穿那件红色的毛衣好看。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Up to now, how many books do you think you've read?
entries.translation_zh: 到目前为止，你认为你阅读过多少书籍？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tell me what you have in your hand.
entries.translation_zh: 告诉我你手里有什么。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You must finish this by 2:30.
entries.translation_zh: 你必须在两点半之前完成它。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She went to Mexico by herself.
entries.translation_zh: 她独自一人去了墨西哥。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I live in a big city.
entries.translation_zh: 我住在一个大城市里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: This is the flag of Japan.
entries.translation_zh: 这是日本国旗。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He behaved as if he were crazy.
entries.translation_zh: 他表现的像个疯子。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I don't like studying in this heat.
entries.translation_zh: 我不喜欢在这么热的气温下念书。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: What time does your class end?
entries.translation_zh: 您几点下课？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You thought I didn't need to do that, didn't you?
entries.translation_zh: 你还以为我不需要那么做，不是吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: This tunnel is twice as long as that one.
entries.translation_zh: 这条隧道是那条的两倍长。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I just wanted to speak to Tom.
entries.translation_zh: 我只想跟汤姆说话。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: They were listening to the radio.
entries.translation_zh: 他们在听收音机。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The doctor told Mary that she couldn't have any more children.
entries.translation_zh: 医生告诉玛丽她不能再要更多孩子了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Can't you stay a little longer?
entries.translation_zh: 你不能待久一点吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Please send me a reply as soon as you receive this mail.
entries.translation_zh: 收到此邮件后请尽快给我答复。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I'm sure you'll love what we have on the menu tonight.
entries.translation_zh: 我肯定你会喜欢我们今晚的菜肴。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I warned you not to come here.
entries.translation_zh: 我警告过你别来这里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I'm glad Tom is on our team.
entries.translation_zh: 我很高兴汤姆在我们队里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I don't believe it at all.
entries.translation_zh: 我根本不信。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She said she had a cold.
entries.translation_zh: 她说她感冒了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The entire sales staff has worked around the clock for a week.
entries.translation_zh: 全体销售人员通宵达旦地工作了一周。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: This river is three times longer than that one.
entries.translation_zh: 这条河比那条长三倍。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You've got a lot of willpower.
entries.translation_zh: 你的意志力很强。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: "Will he pass the examination?" "I am afraid not."
entries.translation_zh: "他会通过考试吗?" "我怕是不会。"
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I don't play tennis that well.
entries.translation_zh: 我网球打得不那么好。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The police will look into the case.
entries.translation_zh: 警察会调查这起案件。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You'd better not go out today.
entries.translation_zh: 你今天最好不要出门。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Give me some paper to write on.
entries.translation_zh: 给我一些纸来写字。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: All sorts of people live in Tokyo.
entries.translation_zh: 各式各样的人住在东京。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom is in the living room.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆在起居室里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: May I be excused from tomorrow's class?
entries.translation_zh: 明天的课我能不来吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There's still no end in sight.
entries.translation_zh: 还是看不见尽头。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I felt that my honor was at stake.
entries.translation_zh: 我觉得我的名誉受到了威胁。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: We make milk into cheese and butter.
entries.translation_zh: 我们把牛奶制成乳酪和奶油。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He painted a picture of roses.
entries.translation_zh: 他画了一幅玫瑰的画。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I advised her to come by 9:00.
entries.translation_zh: 我建议她九点前来。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Our child likes dogs, but I prefer cats.
entries.translation_zh: 我们的孩子喜欢狗，但我更喜欢猫。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She will be seventeen next year.
entries.translation_zh: 她明年将满十七岁。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I saw him sawing a tree.
entries.translation_zh: 我看见他正在锯一棵树。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom got the job on the strength of your recommendation.
entries.translation_zh: 多亏了你的推荐，汤姆得到了那份工作。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There is the train at 10 o'clock.
entries.translation_zh: 十点钟有一班火车。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom had to sell his house.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆必须卖了他的房子。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She is certain to be surprised.
entries.translation_zh: 她肯定会大吃一惊的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom is going to tell Mary he's busy.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆会和玛丽说他很忙。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: This beach is a paradise for surfers.
entries.translation_zh: 这个海滩是冲浪者的天堂。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: These flowers have a unique smell.
entries.translation_zh: 这些花有一种独特的气味。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom helped me with the move.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆帮我搬了家。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I have to repair the refrigerator.
entries.translation_zh: 我要修冰箱。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I accompanied her on the piano.
entries.translation_zh: 我为她作钢琴伴奏。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Please serve him his meal first.
entries.translation_zh: 请先为他上菜。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It'll cost around ten thousand yen.
entries.translation_zh: 它将花费大约10,000日元。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The cat got through the hedge.
entries.translation_zh: 猫从树篱穿过去。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom gave Mary half of the apple.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆给玛丽半个苹果。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: That house with a red roof is my uncle's.
entries.translation_zh: 红色屋顶的房子是我叔叔的屋子。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: They fell into the conversation immediately.
entries.translation_zh: 他们很快就聊起来了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I got this bicycle for nothing.
entries.translation_zh: 我免费得到这辆自行车。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Have you ever dreamed about me?
entries.translation_zh: 你梦见过我吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: We had a lecture on environmental pollution.
entries.translation_zh: 我们有一节关于环境污染的讲座。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: People like you shouldn't be here.
entries.translation_zh: 你这样的人不该在这里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You are as tall as I am.
entries.translation_zh: 你和我一样高。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The car crashed into the truck.
entries.translation_zh: 汽车撞上了卡车。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom no longer knows who I am.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆不知道我是谁
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Smoke is rising from the chimney.
entries.translation_zh: 烟从烟囱上升。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He fell asleep with the radio on.
entries.translation_zh: 他睡著了但收音机还开著。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He is familiar with Japanese culture.
entries.translation_zh: 他熟悉日本文化。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I didn't mean to hurt anybody.
entries.translation_zh: 我没有想要冒犯谁。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I prefer to travel by air.
entries.translation_zh: 我宁愿搭飞机旅行。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Trees do not grow on prairies.
entries.translation_zh: 树不会长在草原上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He was afraid of being laughed at.
entries.translation_zh: 他怕被嘲笑。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Several cottages have been isolated by the flood water.
entries.translation_zh: 好几间小屋被洪水孤立起来了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Go upstairs and bring down my trunk.
entries.translation_zh: 上楼把我的旅行箱拿下来。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I suspect Tom wasn't really surprised.
entries.translation_zh: 我怀疑汤姆没有真的感到惊讶。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It is said that treasure is buried in this area.
entries.translation_zh: 据说这个区域埋着财宝。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom told me that he was headed home.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆告诉我他准备回家了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Can you translate English into Japanese?
entries.translation_zh: 你可以把英文翻译成日语吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She rested her head on her mother's shoulder.
entries.translation_zh: 她把头枕在母亲的肩膀上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: A bicycle will rust if you leave it in the rain.
entries.translation_zh: 如果你把自行车留在雨中，它会生锈的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I encouraged Tom to study harder.
entries.translation_zh: 我鼓励汤姆更努力学习。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You work too hard these days. Aren't you tired?
entries.translation_zh: 这些天你工作太多了。你不累吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I had my bicycle stolen last night.
entries.translation_zh: 昨晚我的自行车被偷了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I wish I earned more money.
entries.translation_zh: 我希望我赚更多的钱。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom refused to help Mary do her homework.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆拒绝帮玛丽做作业。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom can swim faster than anyone else I know.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆是我认识的人当中游泳最快的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I'm surprised Tom hasn't bounced back by now.
entries.translation_zh: 我很惊讶汤姆到现在还没有恢复过来。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He threw a rock into the pond.
entries.translation_zh: 他扔一块石头到池塘里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He put his hand gently on her shoulder.
entries.translation_zh: 他把他的手温柔地放在她的肩上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Mom is preparing a meal in the kitchen.
entries.translation_zh: 妈妈正在厨房里做饭。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Imagination affects every part of our lives.
entries.translation_zh: 想像力影响着我们生活的各个方面。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The train was delayed because of heavy snowfall.
entries.translation_zh: 火车因大雪被耽搁了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You should go and brush your teeth.
entries.translation_zh: 你该去洗牙了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom asked me to shut the gate.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆让我把门关上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You don't need to be afraid of making mistakes.
entries.translation_zh: 你们不需要害怕犯错。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom often fails to keep his word.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆经常不守约。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It rarely snows here in the winter.
entries.translation_zh: 这里的冬天很少下雪。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom says he hopes Mary can do that.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说他希望玛丽能做到。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom is unlikely to do that again.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆不太可能再那么做了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom thinks that Mary may have an eating disorder.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆认为玛丽可能有进食障碍。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: "He used to love me," she answered sadly.
entries.translation_zh: 他以前爱我，她伤心地回答。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Could you wrap this separately, please?
entries.translation_zh: 请你分开包装好吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She likes to go to the sauna.
entries.translation_zh: 她喜欢去桑拿。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom says he doesn't know how to swim.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说他不知道怎么游泳。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The car got stuck in the mud.
entries.translation_zh: 这辆车陷在泥里了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He rushed into the room with his coat on.
entries.translation_zh: 他穿着衣服冲进房间。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I wonder if Tom likes me.
entries.translation_zh: 我不知道汤姆是否喜欢我。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She speaks clearly enough to be easily understood.
entries.translation_zh: 她说得足够清楚明白。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom has lost his interest in studying French.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆已经对学法语不感兴趣了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Hang your coat on the hook.
entries.translation_zh: 把你的外套挂在钩子上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: My car was badly damaged in the accident.
entries.translation_zh: 我的车在事故中受到严重损坏
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He will regret it sooner or later.
entries.translation_zh: 他迟早会后悔。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You nearly poked me in the eye with your pencil.
entries.translation_zh: 你的铅笔差点戳到我的眼睛了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It will cost around 10,000 yen.
entries.translation_zh: 它将花费大约10,000日元。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom says that he's a creature of habit.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说他是习惯的产物。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The path zigzagged up the steep slope.
entries.translation_zh: 这条小路沿著陡峭的斜坡蜿蜒而上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Beginners should learn spoken English first.
entries.translation_zh: 初学者应先学习口说英语。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There are a lot of dictionaries in the bookcase.
entries.translation_zh: 书架上放着很多字典。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I've just eaten some sushi and drunk a beer.
entries.translation_zh: 我刚才吃了寿司喝了啤酒。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Excuse me, do you speak English?
entries.translation_zh: 对不起。你会讲英语吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Mary prefers to dye her hair blonde.
entries.translation_zh: 玛丽更想把头发染成金色。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I think that our living together has influenced your habits.
entries.translation_zh: 我觉得我和你一起住影响了你生活的方式。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Do you think Tom is wealthy?
entries.translation_zh: 你认为汤姆有钱吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom could have dealt with the problem in a better way.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆本可以用更好的方式处理问题。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: In this case, the adjective goes before the noun.
entries.translation_zh: 在这种情况下，形容词放在名词前面。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Scotland is famous for its woollen textiles.
entries.translation_zh: 苏格兰以其羊毛纺织品而著名。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Seeing the fresh lobster made me hungry.
entries.translation_zh: 看见新鲜龙虾，我有了食欲。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The old lady climbed the stairs with difficulty.
entries.translation_zh: 这名老妇艰难地爬上楼梯。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She had a vague feeling of guilt.
entries.translation_zh: 她隐约地有一种的内疚感。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The oranges in this bag are rotten.
entries.translation_zh: 这个袋子里的柳橙都烂了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The explosion may have been caused by a gas leak.
entries.translation_zh: 这场爆炸有可能是瓦斯外泄引起的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: An eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.
entries.translation_zh: 以眼还眼，以牙还牙。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It seems to me that she has a tendency to exaggerate.
entries.translation_zh: 在我看来，她有一种夸张的倾向。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She twisted her ankle while she was doing exercise.
entries.translation_zh: 她锻炼时扭伤了脚踝。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom wasn't smiling when he entered the room.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆进房间时没有微笑。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: They demanded that the mayor should resign.
entries.translation_zh: 他们要求市长辞职。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom would do practically anything to keep Mary happy.
entries.translation_zh: 为了哄玛莉开心，汤姆几乎会做任何事。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: This towel is so soft and fluffy. It feels good!
entries.translation_zh: 这条毛巾蓬松又柔软，特别舒服。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Could you pass me the mashed potatoes?
entries.translation_zh: 你能给我土豆泥吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: To err is human, to forgive divine.
entries.translation_zh: 人皆有错，唯圣者能恕。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You should've rejected such an unfair proposal.
entries.translation_zh: 你本该拒绝一个那么不公平的提议。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Mary carries pepper spray in her purse.
entries.translation_zh: 玛丽的包里有辣椒喷雾。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I ate breakfast on my balcony.
entries.translation_zh: 我在阳台上吃的早饭。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There aren't any medicines that can cure this disease.
entries.translation_zh: 没有药物能够治疗这种疾病。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: What's it like being Tom's girlfriend?
entries.translation_zh: 做汤姆的女朋友怎么样？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The telephone was invented by Bell.
entries.translation_zh: 电话是由贝尔发明的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You needn't have taken an umbrella with you.
entries.translation_zh: 你其实没必要带伞。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom doesn't even know how to fry an egg.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆甚至连怎么煎鸡蛋都不知道。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I should've listened to Tom at that time.
entries.translation_zh: 那时候就该听汤姆的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: People who drink alcoholic beverages are not allowed to enter.
entries.translation_zh: 饮酒者不准禁入。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: All of Tom's predictions came true.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说的预言全部都应验了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He is a dentist by profession.
entries.translation_zh: 他是一位专职牙医。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom admitted that he was the one who ate all the cookies.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆承认是他把所有的饼干都吃光了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The rumor proved to be true.
entries.translation_zh: 经过证实，谣言是真的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Who does this suitcase belong to?
entries.translation_zh: 这个手提箱是谁的?
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom spent more than three years in jail.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆蹲了三年多的监狱。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom made a fool of himself in front of Mary.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆在玛莉面前出丑了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom refused to pay the bill.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆拒绝付账。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: My uncle kept on drinking in spite of his doctor's advice.
entries.translation_zh: 我叔叔不顾医生劝阻，还是继续喝酒。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom put his hand over the microphone.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆把手放在麦克风上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He likes to build model planes.
entries.translation_zh: 他喜欢建造模型飞机。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I refuse to be treated like a slave by you.
entries.translation_zh: 我拒绝被你像奴隶一样对待。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The girl made an awkward bow.
entries.translation_zh: 这个女孩做了一把很难使用的弓。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom has lost interest in studying French.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆已经失去学习法语的兴趣。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I was calm until I saw the syringe.
entries.translation_zh: 看到针管儿之前我一直都很淡定。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom looked at his reflection in the mirror.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆看他在镜中的映像。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The cottage was clean and tidy.
entries.translation_zh: 小屋干净又整洁。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She hired him as a programmer.
entries.translation_zh: 她聘请他作程式设计师。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Slight inattention can cause a great disaster.
entries.translation_zh: 失之毫厘，谬之千里。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: A devastating earthquake hit the state capital.
entries.translation_zh: 一场毁灭性的地震袭击了这个州的首府。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: All of us would like to get rid of nuclear weapons.
entries.translation_zh: 我们所有的人都想除掉核子武器。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom is a jazz singer in Boston.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆是波士顿的爵士歌手。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She made a lot of spelling mistakes.
entries.translation_zh: 她犯了很多拼写错误。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He risked losing all his fortune.
entries.translation_zh: 他冒着失去所有财产的危险。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: They eat dinner at twelve o'clock.
entries.translation_zh: 他们在12点吃晚饭。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom has made a big mistake.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆犯了个大失误。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There's no need to pretend anymore.
entries.translation_zh: 已经不需要再伪装了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom backed out of the deal.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆反悔了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Don't get an upset stomach by eating too much.
entries.translation_zh: 不要因为吃得太多而扰乱胃的正常消化。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She assumed an air of indifference.
entries.translation_zh: 她假装不在意。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He was accused of stealing money.
entries.translation_zh: 他被指控偷钱。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The waves crashed against the rocks.
entries.translation_zh: 浪拍在岩石上。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Have you ever gotten food poisoning?
entries.translation_zh: 您曾经食物中毒过吗？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He confessed that he was guilty.
entries.translation_zh: 他承认有罪。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You can dig dirt from the ground.
entries.translation_zh: 你可以在地上挖土。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: How about some more roast beef?
entries.translation_zh: 再多一些烤牛肉怎么样?
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He amassed a fortune in stock trading during the last boom.
entries.translation_zh: 他在上个景气时期炒股攒下了一笔钱。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom will be the first person to enter the cave.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆将会是第一个进入那洞穴的人。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Air is a mixture of gases.
entries.translation_zh: 空气是气体的混合物。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom has fallen into the river.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆掉进河里了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She wears a lot of makeup.
entries.translation_zh: 她化着浓妆。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The teacher pointed her finger at me and asked me to come with her.
entries.translation_zh: 教师用手指指着我，要我跟她走。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I had been in the army for a month when the war ended.
entries.translation_zh: 战争结束的时候，我已经在军队里待了一个月。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I thought he loved you, but as it is, he loved another girl.
entries.translation_zh: 我以为他爱你，但事实上，他爱另一个女孩。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I would rather live by myself than do as he tells me to do.
entries.translation_zh: 我宁愿自己独立生活也不要他来指使我。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: If you don't have anything nice to say, don't say anything at all.
entries.translation_zh: 如果你没好话说，那就什么都别说。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: "Where have you been?" "I have been to the station to see a friend off."
entries.translation_zh: “你去哪儿了？”“我去了火车站送我的一个朋友。”
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It is hard to see the sights of Tokyo in a few days.
entries.translation_zh: 要看东京的景物只花几天时间是很难的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom didn't know how to translate the word "computer" because the people he was talking to had never seen one.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆不知如何翻译“计算机”一词，因为同他谈话的人从未见过一台。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The other day I went fishing for the first time in my life.
entries.translation_zh: 前几天人生第一次去钓鱼了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: If you heard her speak English, you would take her for an American.
entries.translation_zh: 如果你听到她讲的英语，你会以为她是美国人呢。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Turn the volume up so that the students at the back can hear.
entries.translation_zh: 声音大声一点，以便让坐在后边的学生能听到。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He doesn't seem to be aware of the conflict between my father and me.
entries.translation_zh: 他似乎不知道我父亲和我之间的纠葛。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: We moved our bags to make room for the elderly lady to sit down.
entries.translation_zh: 我们移走了行李腾出空间让老太太可以坐下。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: My father is a native French speaker and my mother is a native English speaker.
entries.translation_zh: 我父亲的母语是法语，我母亲的是英语。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: We're going to eat a lot tonight so I hope you're not on a diet.
entries.translation_zh: 我们今晚会吃很多，所以我希望你没有在节食。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I always enjoy listening to classical music when I have some free time.
entries.translation_zh: 有空的时候，我总喜欢听古典音乐。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: If you want to go, then go. If you don't want to, then it's no big deal.
entries.translation_zh: 如果你想去，就去好了。如果你不想去，那也没什么大不了的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Because of its origins, Canadian English has features of both American and British English.
entries.translation_zh: 由于它的起源，加拿大英语具有美国英语和​​英国英语两者的特色。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The good thing about this electronic dictionary is that it's easy to carry.
entries.translation_zh: 这电子辞典的好处就是便于携带。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: To be very honest with you, I don't think I can do that.
entries.translation_zh: 和你说句实话，我觉得我做不到。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The large crowd roared in approval as Mark Knopfler played the first few bars of "Money for Nothing".
entries.translation_zh: 就像马克·诺弗勒早期演唱的歌曲《金钱无用》一样，绝大多数的人依然高呼赞成“金钱无用论”。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom says he isn't sure if he can do that, but he says he'll try.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说他不确定是否能做到，但是他说会尝试。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom doesn't know what Mary wants him to buy her for her birthday.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆不知道玛莉想要他买什么生日礼物给她。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I would rather go to the art museum than to the movie theater.
entries.translation_zh: 我想去美术馆胜过去电影院。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: There used to be a big pine tree in front of my house.
entries.translation_zh: 以前我家前面有棵大松树。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I've just spoken to your French teacher and he says you're doing well in his class.
entries.translation_zh: 我刚跟你的法语老师谈过话，他说你在课堂上表现得很好。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The best way to lose weight is to eat less and exercise more.
entries.translation_zh: 最好的减肥方法就是少吃多运动。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Whoever said money can't buy happiness simply didn't know where to go shopping.
entries.translation_zh: 那些说钱不能买来幸福的人，只是不知道上哪里去买而已。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Money takes a long time to earn, but you can spend it in no time.
entries.translation_zh: 钱要花很长的时间来赚, 但你可以一下子就花掉它了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I want to move out of this cramped room as soon as I can.
entries.translation_zh: 我想尽快从这个不透气的屋子里出去。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He was aware that I was around, but didn't come to greet me.
entries.translation_zh: 他注意到我在场，但是没向我打招呼。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I knew Tom would be a good basketball coach. That's why I hired him.
entries.translation_zh: 我知道汤姆会成为一位优秀的篮球教练。这就是为什么我聘用了他。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Do you know how far it is from the station to city hall?
entries.translation_zh: 你知道从火车站到市政府有多远？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She had already gone to bed when I phoned her at 11:00 p.m.
entries.translation_zh: 23点我打电话给她的时候，她已经去睡觉了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: After I got married, my Japanese got better and I could understand more.
entries.translation_zh: 在我结婚之后，我的日语水平提高了，能理解更多日语了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: A female friend of mine loves to go to gay bars with me.
entries.translation_zh: 我的一个女性朋友喜欢跟我一起去同志酒吧。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: She had already gone to bed when I called her at 11 p.m.
entries.translation_zh: 23点我打电话给她的时候，她已经去睡觉了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The population of London is much greater than that of any other British city.
entries.translation_zh: 伦敦的人口远远多于其他英国城市的人口。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: We did not expect him to finish the task in so short a time.
entries.translation_zh: 我们没指望过他在那么短的时间里完成工作。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: We'll need a head hunting agency to find the right man for this executive position.
entries.translation_zh: 我们需要人力仲介公司帮我们找到合适的人来担任这个管理职位。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You should apologize to Dad for not coming home in time for supper.
entries.translation_zh: 你应该为你没有及时回家吃晚餐向你父亲道歉。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: If we were supposed to talk more than listen, we would have been given two mouths and one ear.
entries.translation_zh: 如果我们应该少听多说话, 那么我们应该得到两个嘴巴一只耳朵才是。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom said the scenery was more beautiful than he thought it would be.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说，风景比他想像的还要美。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: It's impossible to do the sights of Tokyo in a day or two.
entries.translation_zh: 一两天是没办法逛完东京的。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Dad gave me a book, and my little brother gave me a pen.
entries.translation_zh: 爸爸给了我一本书, 弟弟给了我一枝笔。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I caught a glimpse of Mt. Fuji from the window of the train.
entries.translation_zh: 我从火车车窗瞥见了富士山。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The baby caught sight of my ballpoint pen and reached out for it.
entries.translation_zh: 这个婴儿盯著我的原子笔看, 并且伸手要拿它。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: All of a sudden, the enemy bombs came down on us like rain.
entries.translation_zh: 突然，敌人的炸弹像雨点一样落向我们。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Her eyes shone with joy when she saw that her mother was not mad at her.
entries.translation_zh: 当她看到妈妈没在生她的气，她的双眼因为幸福而闪烁了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom said Mary thought John might be permitted to do that next weekend.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆说玛丽觉得约翰下周末可能被允许那么做。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Ladies and gentlemen, due to an accident at the airport, our arrival will be delayed.
entries.translation_zh: 女士们，先生们，由于机场的意外情况，我们的著陆将被推迟。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: On the table, there was a plate with three fried eggs on it.
entries.translation_zh: 桌子上放著一个盘子，上面放著三颗煎蛋。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: They say you shouldn't take rumors seriously, but that's easier said than done.
entries.translation_zh: 人们常说不要乱相信谣言，但说起来容易做起来难。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Charles Lindbergh made the first solo flight across the Atlantic Ocean in 1927.
entries.translation_zh: Charles Lindbergh于1927年成功完成了第一次独自飞越大西洋。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Today, I was supposed to study at the library but I woke up around 12 o'clock.
entries.translation_zh: 今天我本打算在图书馆学习，但到12点左右才醒。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Because of the problem of air pollution, the bicycle may some day replace the automobile.
entries.translation_zh: 因为空气污染的问题，也许有一天自行车会取代汽车。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Tom has been to this park with Mary at least a dozen times.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆跟玛丽来这公园至少十二次。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: At lunchtime today, our usual restaurant was closed because of a funeral in the family.
entries.translation_zh: 今天午餐时间，我们常去的饭馆关门了，因为他们家在办丧事。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He was an excellent scientist, and what is more, was a great poet.
entries.translation_zh: 他是一位优秀的科学家，更是一位伟大的诗人。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I forgot to tell Tom to take the cookies out of the oven.
entries.translation_zh: 我忘了告诉汤姆把饼干从烤箱拿出来。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The only difference between a bad cook and a poisoner is the intent.
entries.translation_zh: 一个差劲的厨师和下毒者唯一的不同在于他们的意图。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: If a sick person folds one thousand paper cranes, her wish will come true.
entries.translation_zh: 如果一个病人折一千只纸鹤, 她的愿望就会成真。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: My fingers are so numb with cold that I can't play the piano.
entries.translation_zh: 我的手指冻僵了，以至于无法弹钢琴了。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I crossed the equator for the first time on my trip to Australia.
entries.translation_zh: 我去澳大利亚旅游的时候第一次穿越了赤道。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: After Tom lost his job, he started to gamble to cope with his depression.
entries.translation_zh: 汤姆失业后，为了排遣郁闷的心情而开始了赌博。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: You always wear a ponytail. Why not try pigtails every now and then?
entries.translation_zh: 你总是绑马尾辫，为什么不偶尔试试双马尾呢？
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I've never lived on a farm, but both of my parents grew up on farms.
entries.translation_zh: 我从没有在农场住，但我的父母都在农场长大。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: He is a man of few words, but he always keeps his promise.
entries.translation_zh: 他这个人话不多，但总是遵守诺言。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Kindness is the language which the deaf can hear and the blind can see.
entries.translation_zh: 善良是聋子能听盲人能看的语言。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I was busy packing, because I was leaving for France in two days.
entries.translation_zh: 我当时正忙着打包，因为我两天后要去法国。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The ecological footprint of a country is closely related to its level of economic development.
entries.translation_zh: 生态印迹与一国的经济发展程度密切相关。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: After he had graduated from the university, he taught English for two years.
entries.translation_zh: 从他大学毕业以后, 他教了两年的英语。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: I think it's dangerous to climb a mountain on a day when it's stormy.
entries.translation_zh: 我认为在有风暴的时候里登山很危险。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The crash was and remains to date the deadliest airplane accident on Canadian soil.
entries.translation_zh: 这起空难是迄今为止加拿大境内最严重的空难。
entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: When Dad finds out what you've done, he's going to hit the roof.

entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: Mary tied an apron around her waist and then took the turkey out of the oven.

entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: The skin of a shark is much rougher than that of a tuna fish.

entries.source_title: Tatoeba sentence pairs (eng-cmn)

entries.text: In London, the police are always worried about finding a bomb on the train or subway.

entries.text: Thus we know that many of the Jews of Poland came to Russia as a troublesome legacy on the division of that kingdom.

entries.text: It is with reluctance that the average elector acknowledges--if he can be brought to acknowledge at all--that any public office requires special qualifications.

entries.text: Whence it followed that there was no demand for prohibitive liquor laws, and therefore only failure had resulted from attempting to enforce them.

entries.text: But as the person converted by the example would be certain not to parade the fact, no statistics could even then be attainable.
